entries.definition:
r–b–w hill, elevated part of the land, to go on the top of a hill;
growth, to increase; usury; to be out of breath, to have asthma; to
raise, to grow under someone’s care, to educate, to cultivate; a
group of ten thousand people. Of this root, eight forms occur 20
times in the Qur’an: رَبَا rabā four times; رَبَّى rabbā twice; أَرْبَى
ʾarbā (1) once; رَابِي rābī once; رَابِيَة rābiyatun once; أَرْبَى ʾarbā (2)
once; رِبًا ribā eight times and رَبْوَة rabwatun twice.

رَبَا rabā ū [v. intrans.] 1 to increase, to grow (30:39) وَمَا آتَيْتُمْ مِنْ
رِبًا لِيَرْبُوَ فِي أَمْوَالِ النَّاسِ فَلا يَرْبُو عِنْدَ اللَّهِ whatever you give out in usury
to increase in value through [other] people’s wealth will not
increase with God 2 to swell up, to come to life (22:5) وَتَرَى الْأَرْضَ
هَامِدَةً فَإِذَا أَنْزَلْنَا عَلَيْهَا الْمَاءَ اهْتَزَّتْ وَرَبَتْ you perceive the earth lifeless,
yet when We send down upon it water, it stirs and swells.

رَبَّى rabbā [v. II, trans.] to bring up, to raise, to rear (17:24) وَقُلْ

346
